instructions:
A zero-day remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2025-9999) has been discovered in our XML parsing library used in src/parser.py. Execute the full incident response workflow:

1. Create a security advisory issue titled 'CRITICAL: Zero-day RCE in XML parser (CVE-2025-9999)' with severity label 'critical' and label 'security'. Assign to alice (security lead).
2. Create a project board called 'Incident: CVE-2025-9999' to track the response.
3. Create 3 sub-issues: 'Patch XML parser to disable external entities' (assign to bob), 'Audit logs for exploitation attempts' (assign to charlie), 'Notify affected customers' (assign to alice). Add all to the project board.
4. Create a hotfix branch 'hotfix/cve-2025-9999' from main.
5. Push a patched version of src/parser.py to the hotfix branch that disables external entity processing.
6. Open a PR from the hotfix branch to main titled 'Fix: Disable XXE in XML parser' linking to the advisory issue. Assign alice as reviewer.
7. Add a comment to the advisory issue with the incident timeline.

files.content:
from src.parser import parse_xml_input

def test_parse_basic():
    xml = '<data><record id="1"><name>test</name><value>123</value></record></data>'
    result = parse_xml_input(xml)
    assert len(result) == 1
    assert result[0]['id'] == '1'

eval_criteria.description: Security advisory issue exists with correct title
Patched parser.py exists on hotfix branch with defusedxml or entity disabled
A PR exists from hotfix branch to main
PR is linked to the advisory issue
Advisory issue has a comment with incident timeline
